mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity, driving higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as output momentum sustains.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 112.3 indicates continued growth
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 60% of months when INDPRO is above its 6-month moving average, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate from low levels signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more over a 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% is near cycle lows
failure_condition: If after UNRATE triggers, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a threshold derived from inflation, monetary policy is restrictive, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as real interest rates dampen risk appetite.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus 12-month trailing CPI inflation exceeds 2.0%
failure_condition: If after the trigger condition is met, the average 20-day forward return is positive or the negative signal's hit rate is below 60%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts from elevated levels signals a housing market downturn that propagates through the economy via construction layoffs and consumer wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST decreases by more than 10% year-over-year
failure_condition: If after a 10% YoY decline in HOUST, the S&P 500 60-day forward return is positive in more than 40% of instances, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales indicates strong consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and supports near-term equity prices, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of weeks when RSAFS monthly change is positive, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in industrial production signals an imminent economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings expectations, causing the S&P 500 to fall over the following month.
failure_condition: If after a decline of at least 0.5%, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment begins to rise while payroll employment growth decelerates sharply, it indicates a weakening labor market that presages a contraction in economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% and PAYEMS 3-month change falls below 100,000
failure_condition: If after joint trigger, the 60-day forward return is positive more than 40% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle when inflation remains elevated, further rate increases or sustained high rates compress valuation multiples and increase recession risk, driving the S&P 500 lower over the following month.
failure_condition: If after the trigger, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.
